data.title: Jack in the Box wins the support in ISS in its major board battle
data.description:
Jack in the Box (JACK) won the support of proxy firm ISS in its board battle with Biglari Capital. ISS highlighted that Biglari's focus on removing David Goebel from the board does not fully account for adverse macroeconomic developments over the period in question.

"The combination of constructive shareholder engagement, appropriate refreshment, and the circumstances and timeline surrounding the planned departure of the board chair, who is the sole target of the dissident in this campaign, suggests that the change sought by the dissident is not warranted at this time," updated ISS.

Biglari Capital owns about 9.9% of Jack in the Box (JACK) and previously pushed for board seats, prompting Jack in the Box (JACK) to adopt a short-duration rights plan to cap the ownership stake. After the board declined to add him as a director, Sardar Biglari withdrew his nominees and shifted to a "vote-no" campaign solely against Goebel, arguing change is needed at the top of the board. The company characterized the effort as a “distracting proxy contest” that serves Biglari’s interests over those of all shareholders and stressed Goebel’s restaurant experience as critical to the turnaround.

The Jack in the Box board vote is scheduled for February 27.

Shares of Jack in the Box (JACK) are down more than 40% on a year-to-date basis but have perked up in 2026.

data.title: Hillman Solutions outlines $1.65B revenue target for 2026 while advancing Pro channel and M&A strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Hillman Solutions Corp. (HLMN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Jon Adinolfi, President and CEO, emphasized Hillman's record performance in 2025, stating "net sales increased 5.4% to $1.552 billion, and adjusted EBITDA increased 13.9% to $275.3 million versus 2024." He highlighted the team's success navigating tariff impacts and achieving the best year in the company's 62-year history.
  • Adinolfi shared that for 2026, the company estimates full-year net sales between $1.6 billion and $1.7 billion, with a midpoint of $1.65 billion, representing 6.3% top-line growth over 2025, and expects to generate adjusted EBITDA between $275 million and $285 million. Free cash flow is projected between $100 million and $120 million. He explained, "The main contributor to our top line growth during 2026 will be the rollover from pricing that went into effect during 2025. Our sales team is focused on winning new business, and we are confident that new business wins during 2026 will outpace last year."
  • Adinolfi noted no meaningful macro tailwinds expected in 2026 and called out the acquisition of Intex DIY in August 2024 and steady market share gains. He highlighted that Hardware and Protective Solutions (HPS) net sales grew 7.8% to $1.2 billion, with adjusted EBITDA up 26% to $196.3 million, while Robotics and Digital Solutions (RDS) saw 1.6% sales growth. The company completed over 1,800 MinuteKey 3.5 kiosk installations and is on track for further rollouts in 2026.
  • Adinolfi described a strategic focus on expanding the Pro channel, stating, "We have recently assembled an experienced team with deep Pro knowledge that is focused on growing our Pro business." He signaled a healthy M&A pipeline and upcoming Investor Day details.
  • CFO Robert Kraft said, "Fourth quarter 2025 net sales increased 4.5% to $365.1 million versus the prior year quarter... For the full year 2025, adjusted gross profit margin increased 60 basis points to 48.7%." Kraft highlighted operating cash flow of $105 million in 2025 and free cash flow of $35.1 million, noting a $65 million tariff impact. He reported a year-end net debt of $665.8 million and available liquidity of $306 million. The company invested $70 million in capital expenditures and repurchased 1.4 million shares for $12.4 million.

Outlook

  • Hillman projects 2026 net sales between $1.6 billion and $1.7 billion, with the midpoint reflecting 6.3% growth over 2025. Adjusted EBITDA is expected between $275 million and $285 million. Free cash flow is forecast between $100 million and $120 million. Adjusted gross margins are anticipated to be between 46% and 47% due to fully realized tariff pricing and costs.
  • Kraft explained the guidance assumes a combination of new business wins and a mid-single-digit contribution from price, with high-end guidance based on flat market volumes. He stated, "We expect our 2026 net sales growth to outpace our 2026 adjusted EBITDA growth."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q4 2025 were $365.1 million, a 4.5% increase over the prior year quarter. Full-year 2025 net sales reached $1.552 billion. Adjusted gross profit margin in Q4 was 47.6%, down 10 basis points year-over-year, while full-year adjusted gross profit margin rose to 48.7% from 48.1%. Adjusted SG&A as a percentage of sales in Q4 increased to 31.8%. Adjusted EBITDA in Q4 was $57.5 million, up 2.3%. For the full year, adjusted EBITDA grew 13.9% to $275.3 million, with adjusted EBITDA margin at 17.7%.
  • Operating activities generated $105 million during 2025, compared to $183 million in 2024. Free cash flow was $35.1 million, including a $65 million tariff impact. Year-end net debt was $665.8 million, and net debt to trailing 12-month adjusted EBITDA ratio improved to 2.4x.

Q&A

  • Lee Jagoda, CJS Securities: Asked about gross margin cadence and new business wins for 2026. Kraft responded that Q1 will be the low point for gross margin, likely below the 46%-47% range, and sequential improvement is expected throughout the year. Adinolfi expressed confidence in new business wins, citing "some nice wins that we're building off of in '25 that will cascade into '26."
  • W. Andrew Carter, Stifel: Inquired about Protective Solutions sales deterioration and RDS headwinds. Adinolfi attributed Protective Solutions softness to near-term dynamics and inventory balancing, while RDS's customer transition will continue in Q1 and Q2 with anticipated growth thereafter.
  • Stephen Volkmann, Jefferies: Asked about operating leverage if existing home sales rebound. Kraft indicated incremental EBITDA margins would be "plus 20%" for most of the business and "plus 30%" for RDS. Adinolfi said Canada remains under pressure but expects improvement in 2026.
  • David Manthey, Baird: Sought clarification on long-term 6% and 10% revenue and EBITDA growth targets. Kraft stated these will be addressed at the March Investor Day. Manthey also asked about chip shortages, and Kraft replied, "I've not heard anything from our teams around chip issues, and I don't think we expect that, that will be a challenge going forward."
  • Brian McNamara, Canaccord: Queried about the step-down in guidance and M&A environment. Kraft explained the guidance revision reflects softer early 2026 trends. Adinolfi noted increased M&A opportunities, saying, "We feel confident we'll do 1 to 2 deals in 2026."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was neutral to slightly cautious, focusing on margin cadence, new business wins, RDS transitions, and M&A opportunities. Questions on softer guidance and segment performance reflected a desire for clarity rather than skepticism.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, highlighting resilience and growth drivers, but adopted a more measured and pragmatic stance when discussing margin normalization and early-year softness. Phrases like "we feel really good about the business" and "we are confident we have the right to win" showcased ongoing optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence remained strong, with slightly more emphasis on normalization and prudent guidance. Analyst tone shifted from cautiously optimistic to seeking explanations for guidance moderation and segment-specific headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 net sales at a midpoint of $1.65 billion (6.3% growth) was specified, compared to the prior quarter's directional guidance of high single to low double digits. Adjusted EBITDA growth guidance was moderated to 1.7% at the midpoint, reflecting expected margin normalization after a strong 2025.
  • Management's messaging evolved from highlighting record outperformance and macro optimism in Q3 to a more cautious approach in Q4, citing market volume softness and fully realized tariff impacts.
  • Analyst focus shifted from competitive positioning and pricing elasticity in Q3 to guidance cadence, RDS headwinds, and M&A pipeline in Q4. Both quarters saw questions on new business wins, but Q4 included more discussion on specific segment trends and operational leverage.
  • While confidence in the business model and new initiatives (Pro channel, M&A) persisted, the language around market conditions and guidance became more conservative.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified ongoing macro headwinds, particularly weak existing home sales, as a drag on home improvement demand. Adinolfi stated, "Existing home sales remained soft and unchanged from the 30-year lows we saw during 2024, totaling $4.06 million."
  • Tariffs continued to impact costs and cash flow, with $65 million of tariff impact noted in 2025. Management expects margin normalization in 2026 as tariff pricing and costs are fully realized.
  • Analysts raised concerns about gross margin cadence, Protective Solutions performance, RDS customer transitions, and the implications of guidance moderation. Management addressed these by outlining inventory dynamics, new product launches, and ongoing investments in technology and teams.

Final Takeaway

Hillman Solutions closed 2025 with record net sales and profit, driven by disciplined execution amid tariff and volume challenges. For 2026, management projects continued top-line growth to a midpoint of $1.65 billion, expansion in the Pro channel, and a robust M&A pipeline, while signaling prudent expectations for margin normalization and cautious optimism on market conditions. The company remains focused on operational excellence, customer care, and capitalizing on new business opportunities, positioning itself for further strategic growth despite macro headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Galaxy Digital CEO: Bitcoin faces competition from sports betting, remains generational asset
data.description:
Characterizing the current trading environment for Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a "bear market," Galaxy Digital (GLXY) CEO Michael Novogratz said the digital asset faced competition from other speculative endeavors, including sports betting.

However, the famed cryptocurrency investor also reiterated his belief that Bitcoin remains a "generational asset," which will get a boost from lower interest rates and continued adoption by the investing community.

"We’re in this transition now where there are other speculative assets that people are playing from sports betting to gold and silver to equity markets. And so we’ve got competition for that wallet," Novogratz said during his company's recent earnings call.

Still, the Galaxy Digital CEO took a bullish view of Bitcoin's long-term future. "We believe that Bitcoin is going to be a generational asset," he said. "That doesn’t mean that at $130[K], I wish I didn’t sell half of it to buy it back at $60[K], but it’s hard to be a gold company without owning gold and believing in gold."

The crypto investor added: "Bitcoin has been our symbolic asset that demonstrated confidence in that future. And so it’s hard when Bitcoin goes down for employees to say, oh, s***. But we haven’t had anyone flinch, right?"

Looking ahead, Novogratz pointed to Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee as the next Federal Reserve Chair, as a potential near-term catalyst. He also noted that Bitcoin (BTC-USD) still has potential to become more prominent in investment portfolios.

"Right now, [Warsh is] dovish, right? He believes that AI is going to crush inflation and that we have inflation heading lower and therefore, rates are too high, he’s going to cut rates," Novogratz said.

Commenting on the attitude toward Bitcoin (BTC-USD) from the overall investment community, the Galaxy Digital CEO stated: "There are 200 million-plus people that own Bitcoin around the world. Institutions are moving into it. It doesn’t have a huge allocation in portfolios yet."

Bitcoin dropped from an all-time high above $126K late last year to a level near $60K last month. The cryptocurrency has recovered slightly from those recent lows, although it continues to hover below $70K.

The slide in Bitcoin has impacted a host of companies with close association with the cryptocurrency. Galaxy Digital (GLXY) is down about 6% so far in 2026. There have also been notable declines in stocks like Coinbase (COIN), Circle (CRCL), Strategy (MSTR), MARA Holdings (MARA) and Bit Digital (BTBT).

data.title: Mining shares slide as gold slumps below $4,900, silver under $75
data.description:
Gold and silver futures fall sharply in thin trading Tuesday, with much of Asia closed for the Lunar New Year and U.S. markets shut on Monday; April Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) -3.1% at $4,886/oz and March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) -6.4% at $72.97/oz.

Among many precious metals stocks showing steep losses: First Majestic Silver (AG) -9.3%, Seabridge Gold (SA) -8.9%, Sibanye-Stillwater (SBSW) -8.7%, Endeavour Silver (EXK) -8.5%, Hecla Mining (HL) -8.5%, Silvercorp Metals (SVM) -8.5%, Hudbay Minerals (HBM) -8.2%, Alamos Gold (AGI) -7.8%, Iamgold (IAG) -7.7%, Coeur Mining (CDE) -7.2%, Eldorado Gold (EGO) -6.8%, Pan American Silver (PAAS) -6.7%, Gold Fields (GFI) -6.6%, Goldmining Inc. (GLDG) -5.6%, Anglogold Ashanti (AU) -5.5%, Barrick Gold (B) -5.4%, Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) -5.2%, Newmont (NEM) -5.1%, Gold Royalty (GROY) -5.1%, Kinross Gold (KGC) -5%, Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) -4.2%, Franco-Nevada (FNV) -3.1%.

Scheduled talks between the U.S. and Iran have raised cautious optimism that diplomatic channels remain open, DHF Capital's Bas Kooijman said in a note. "Any tangible de-escalation could redirect flows toward risk assets and temper safe-haven demand."

Also, expected talks between Russia and Ukraine have created tentative hopes of progress, even as hostilities continue, Kooijman added.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Mitt signals $35M capital rotation target through call rights as home equity focus drives 2026 outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TPG Mortgage Investment Trust, Inc. (MITT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Thomas Durkin reported, "During the fourth quarter, we saw book value remain stable, increasing from $10.46 to $10.48 and we produced an EAD of $0.25, covering our most recently declared dividend of $0.23." Durkin also stated, "For the full year 2025, we were able to increase our quarterly dividend 3x by a total of over 21% and delivered a 6.5% economic return on equity. Most important, MITT's total return to shareholders, including dividends and stock price appreciation through today is a standout 42%."
  • Durkin identified three strategic objectives for 2026: resolving legacy WMC CRE loans in the first half, building on Arc Home's earnings momentum, and capital rotation through legacy deals becoming callable.
  • Nicholas Smith, Chief Investment Officer, noted, "For the full year 2025, we grew our investment portfolio 27% compared to 2024, ending the year at $8.5 billion. This growth was driven by over $3 billion in total loan purchases throughout the year." Smith highlighted, "In Q4, we remained highly active securitizing $1.3 billion. This included partnering with top mortgage originators on 2 home equity securitizations totaling $960 million where we retained $55 million of securities."
  • Smith emphasized Arc Home's performance, stating, "Arc Home originated over $3.4 billion for the year 2025. The strong earnings at Arc Home, driven by steady gain on sale margins and high lock volumes have positively contributed to our earnings available for distribution."
  • CFO Anthony Rossiello stated, "Book value increased by 0.2% during the fourth quarter to $10.48 per share. Including our $0.23 dividend, we generated a 2.4% economic return for our shareholders. GAAP net income available to common shareholders was $8 million or $0.25 per share, primarily driven by EAD as net unrealized gains on our investment portfolio were partially offset by transaction-related expenses."

Outlook

  • Management aims to resolve the legacy WMC CRE loans in the first half of 2026 and reinvest approximately $28 million of equity to bolster earnings.
  • Smith indicated, "Looking forward to 2026, we intend to remain aggressive in exercising call rights on in-the-money securitizations to return capital that can be opportunistically redeployed in our core, higher returning investment strategies. We see significant EAD upside in rotating approximately $35 million of equity this year."
  • Durkin reiterated, "We have consistently executed on our stated objectives and believe we have clear line of sight into more powerful ROEs and EAD as we look ahead into 2026."

Financial Results

  • Rossiello reported, "During the quarter, we sponsored 3 securitizations and continue deploying capital into our home equity portfolio. This investment activity, coupled with sustained strength in origination volumes at Arc Home delivered a strong economic return and earnings available for distribution that exceeded our increased dividend level."
  • Net interest income increased by 4% in Q4. Rossiello added, "Net interest income, inclusive of interest earned on our hedge portfolio was $0.68, which exceeded $0.45 of operating expenses and preferred dividends to generate net earnings of $0.23 per share."
  • Arc Home contributed an additional $0.02 per share to EAD in the quarter.
  • For the full year 2025, EAD was $0.86 per share, covering annual dividends of $0.85. Arc Home contributed $1.9 million to EAD in 2025, compared to a loss of $3.3 million in 2024.
  • MITT ended the quarter with total liquidity of $109 million.

Q&A

  • Crispin Love, Piper Sandler: Asked for more detail on Arc Home's Q1 performance and gain on sale margins. Nicholas Smith clarified, "That was their individual profitability. So you have to take into consideration Arc Home's ownership of Arc," while Rossiello explained, "As a niche originator in a space where there's a lot of demand, margins have been healthy, and we've been able to pass that on...volumes have been continuing to increase sequentially."
  • Love also queried incremental capital allocation. Rossiello responded that home equity remains the core focus and performance has been strong.
  • Douglas Harter, UBS: Asked about tightened spreads and why this hasn't increased book value. Rossiello replied, "There has been some drag on IOs from...acquisitions in the past. So residuals just faster speeds into the slightly lower nominal yields and much tighter credit spreads."
  • Trevor Cranston, Citizens: Sought details on call rights and unlevered home equity loans. Rossiello said, "In the prepared remarks, we mentioned that there were 2 transactions we were focused on, which free up, call it, $35-ish million of equity...the rest will come through in subsequent quarters." On unlevered loans, Rossiello explained they are held as a cash substitute against favorable financing.
  • Bose George, KBW: Inquired about accretion from WMC as debt capital rolls off and competitive dynamics in non-QM. Durkin stated, "We have $28 million of equity...converting them to whether it's 15% or 20% ROE is, we think, is worth approximately $0.20 on an annualized basis as we're able to rotate that $28 million in full." Rossiello said, "As more and more brokers get familiarity with this product, we see the pie growing."
  • Matthew Erdner, JonesTrading: Asked about ROEs in securitizations and expected pace. Rossiello said, "Our ROEs on securitizations, we believe we are clocking in a decent amount higher than that, call it anywhere from 5% to 10% given sort of the unique way we're catching the marketplace."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed an inquisitive but neutral tone, asking for deeper clarification on capital allocation, profitability, and book value drivers, with no confrontational exchanges.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing execution and growth, with statements like, "We have consistently executed on our stated objectives...clear line of sight into more powerful ROEs and EAD."
  • Compared to Q3, analyst tone remains steady and constructive, while management's confidence appears slightly heightened due to improved performance metrics and expansion plans.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language now places increased emphasis on capital rotation from legacy assets and call rights, while the prior quarter focused more on initial successes in Arc Home and portfolio growth.
  • Strategic focus has shifted toward rapid deployment of freed-up capital and scaling profitable niche segments such as home equity and non-QM originations.
  • Management's tone is more assertive regarding future ROE and EAD growth, in contrast to a more measured optimism previously.
  • Key metrics reveal ongoing increases in book value, dividend, and EAD, while Arc Home has fully transitioned from a loss contributor to a profit generator.
  • Analysts continue to focus on asset allocation, ROE, and securitization cadence, with question themes largely consistent quarter-over-quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management referenced the ongoing process of resolving legacy WMC CRE loans, noting $28 million of equity remains tied up in these assets, which are on nonaccrual status.
  • There is acknowledgment of potential headwinds due to MITT's smaller cap status, but Durkin argued these are outweighed by the company’s earnings initiatives.
  • Analysts inquired about competitive dynamics in non-QM space and possible impairment risks as capital is redeployed from WMC legacy assets.
  • Rossiello addressed competition by highlighting Arc Home’s ability to absorb increasing supply and benefit from growing market familiarity.

Final Takeaway

MITT enters 2026 with stable book value, growing dividends, and a strategic focus on capital rotation from legacy CRE and residential assets into higher-return segments such as home equity and non-QM loans. The company is targeting the redeployment of approximately $35 million through call rights, expects continued EAD growth, and is leveraging Arc Home’s momentum to drive earnings. Management remains confident in achieving greater ROEs and value creation for shareholders as it executes on these stated initiatives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Enlight outlines record 2026 construction and raises 2028 run-rate target to $2.3B while expanding storage portfolio
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Enlight Renewable Energy Ltd (ENLT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Adi Leviatan described 2025 as “another record year for Enlight,” highlighting a 46% year-over-year increase in both quarterly and full-year revenue and income to $152 million and $582 million, respectively. Leviatan emphasized, “We are operating in a uniquely favorable environment for the energy sector, structural tailwinds, reindustrialization, electrification, and rapidly rising power demand from data centers are driving unprecedented long-term growth across global electricity markets.” He noted that the company exceeded full-year revenue and EBITDA guidance by 4% and 7%, respectively.

  • Leviatan reported the expansion of the total portfolio by 26% to 38 factored gigawatts and a 30% increase in the operating portfolio. He stated, “In Q4, two major U.S. projects, Quail Ranch and Roadrunner achieved COD ahead of schedule, delivering over 800 factored megawatts combined at approximately 13% unlevered returns.”

  • The company started construction on projects totaling 2.6 factored gigawatts during the year, with the most significant addition being the CO Bar 1 and 2 projects, forming part of a 2.4 factored gigawatt flagship complex. Leviatan detailed, “Our mature storage portfolio globally reached 17.5 gigawatt hour. An increase of over 50% from the previous quarter and over 6x its size, just 3 years ago.”

  • Acquisition of Project Jupiter in Germany, a 2-gigawatt hour energy storage project, was highlighted as a major move in Europe, expected to generate approximately 15% unlevered returns. Leviatan announced, “Our mature storage portfolio represents annual run rate revenues of approximately $1 billion.”

  • CFO Nir Yehuda stated, “The fourth quarter of '25 has been a strong quarter for Enlight, mainly resulting from the operation of new projects in the U.S. as we continue to materialize our growth plan.” Yehuda detailed, “In the fourth quarter of '25, the company's total revenues and income increased to $152 million, up from $104 million last year, a growth rate of 46% year-over-year.”

Outlook

  • The company expects 2026 revenues and income between $755 million and $785 million, and adjusted EBITDA between $545 million and $565 million. Yehuda said, “reflecting annual growth of 32% and 27% at the midpoint, respectively, compared to '25 results.”

  • Leviatan projected adding about 1.1 factored gigawatt to operational capacity by end of 2026, which is expected to contribute annual run rate revenue and income of $137 million and adjusted EBITDA of $109 million.

  • By year-end 2028, Enlight expects to achieve 12 to 13 factored gigawatts of operating capacity, generating run-rate revenue and income of $2.1 billion to $2.3 billion. Leviatan noted, “Compared to our estimates in the previous quarter, 2028 revenue and income annual run rate increased by approximately $150 million and the planned capacity expanded by 1 factored gigawatt at the low end.”

  • The unlevered return on investment for under construction and preconstruction projects is now expected to range from 12% to 13%, up from 11% to 12% previously, with a targeted return on equity of more than 18%.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenues and income reached $152 million, driven by $124 million in electricity sales and $28 million in income from tax benefit. New U.S. projects contributed $18 million to revenue growth, including Atrisco, Square Range, and Roadrunner. Project Pupin in Serbia added $5 million, with additional gains from Israeli electricity trade and currency effects.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 reached $99 million, up 51%. Net income for the quarter was $21 million, an increase of $13 million compared to Q4 2024.

  • Enlight secured $4.3 billion in new funding during 2025, including $2.9 billion in project finance, $470 million in tax equity, and $350 million in mezzanine loans, as well as $300 million in equity and $245 million in debenture at the corporate level.

Q&A

  • Justin Clare, ROTH Capital Partners, LLC: Asked about the drivers behind the 2028 revenue outlook increase and the role of acquisitions. CEO Leviatan explained, “The acquisition of the Jupiter project contributed in and of itself. $150 million to the overall sum of the 2028 run rate revenues…we are always looking at opportunities also for acquisitions of projects and pipelines where it makes sense.”

  • Clare inquired about safe harboring capacity. Leviatan responded they plan to safe harbor an additional 0.5 to 3.5 factored gigawatts in H1 2026, with further battery storage safe harboring possible for three more years. McKee added, “The 14 to 17 factored gigawatt range is something that we're actually very proud of.”

  • Mark W. Strouse, JPMorgan: Asked about potential platform acquisitions. Leviatan said, “We do have the flexibility and the ability to raise significant amounts of funds…we're always looking at opportunities to acquire not only projects and platforms of projects, but also potentially if we need those missing capabilities.”

  • Maheep Mandloi, Mizuho Securities: Queried the impact of new safe harbor rules. McKee replied, “They are in line with our previous guidelines and our estimates, and they help reduce uncertainty somewhat.”

  • Mandloi also asked about capital needs through 2028. Itay Banayan stated, “We have all of the available sources in our hands to fund the growth that we're presenting through the end of 2028.”

  • Michael Mcnulty, Deutsche Bank: Asked about expectations for partial asset sales in 2026. Banayan said, “It is part of our strategy to contemplate minority sales or sell-downs of some of our projects where it makes sense and where it's accretive to the company.”

  • Mcnulty further queried growth drivers for 2026 guidance. Leviatan explained, “The U.S. projects that we just connected in Q4 of 2025, Quail Ranch and Roadrunner are going to have their first full year of revenues in 2026.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on understanding drivers of upgraded guidance, growth through acquisitions, safe harbor strategy, and capital sufficiency, displaying a positive and inquisitive tone.

  • Management maintained a confident and proactive tone, emphasizing funding flexibility, project execution, and increased visibility. Leviatan frequently referenced “record year,” “strong finish,” and “positioned for a record construction year in 2026.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained a positive outlook, though the current call featured more detailed questions about M&A and capital allocation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is materially higher, with revenue and income forecasted up to $785 million and adjusted EBITDA up to $565 million, compared to previous full-year guidance of up to $565 million revenue and $415 million adjusted EBITDA.

  • The 2028 outlook has increased, with a $150 million uplift in run-rate revenue and additional gigawatt of capacity at the low end, largely attributed to the Jupiter acquisition and advancing CO Bar 4 and 5.

  • Management tone remains confident, with a continued focus on expansion and risk management. Analysts’ questions have shifted to deeper discussions on growth levers, acquisitions, and capital strategy.

  • Major strategic focus includes storage portfolio expansion, particularly in Europe, and continued U.S. project execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the need for continued execution on construction timelines, safe harboring for tax credits, and ongoing regulatory developments.

  • Analysts raised questions about the ability to fund growth through 2028, the impact of new safe harbor rules, and the potential for additional asset sales.

  • Management described mitigation strategies, including securing diverse funding sources, maintaining a flexible approach to acquisitions, and proactively managing tax incentive eligibility.

Final Takeaway

Enlight Renewable Energy delivered a record 2025, exceeding guidance and significantly expanding its project and storage portfolios. With $4.3 billion in new funding and a robust construction pipeline, the company projects strong growth in 2026 and raised its 2028 revenue and capacity targets. Management stressed confidence in funding capacity through 2028, highlighted the accretive impact of the Jupiter acquisition, and reinforced the focus on disciplined, high-return growth across geographies and technologies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Devon Energy Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Devon Energy (DVN) is set to post fourth-quarter results on Tuesday, after markets close.

Wall Street expects the Oklahoma City-based company to post EPS of $0.83, down 28.4%, while revenue is expected to fall 18% to $3.61 billion during the quarter.

The oil and gas firm, in November, forecast higher output for the fourth quarter after posting profit above estimates. The stock has risen nearly 22% so far this year, compared to the marginal fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Analysts are mostly bullish on Devon, with Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street rating the stock a Buy.

J.P. Morgan analyst Ayun Jayaram upgraded DVN to Overweight from Neutral in part due to an attractive relative valuation, which benefits from free cash flow accretion associated with the company's $1 billion business optimization plan.

The analyst sees well productivity remaining consistent in 2026-27, reflecting a steadier mix of secondary zones relative to the 2025 program.

Devon Energy was in the news after the company and Coterra Energy (CTRA) agreed to merge in an all-stock transaction.

Barclays upgraded the U.S. shale producer, saying its “transformative” merger with Coterra “creates a differentiated E&P rate-of-change story driven by synergy capture, portfolio optimization, and a step-function improvement in long-term cash returns.” The brokerage added that the combined entity “will be too cheap and too large to ignore.”

Over the last two years, Devon has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are bullish and rated the stock a Buy. In contrast, Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider it a Hold.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen four upward revisions, compared to 12 downward revisions. Revenue estimates have been revised upwards four times versus two downward moves.

data.title: SEALSQ expects wider net loss for FY 2025
data.description:
SEALSQ (LAES) expects preliminary revenue of $18M for full year 2025, up 66% year over year, and a net loss of $30M to $40M, wider than its FY2024 net loss of $21M.

The wider net loss reflects further investment in R&D, and sales & marketing efforts designed to accelerate growth with the launch of the new post-quantum semiconductor product range, increased operating expenses due to the consolidation of IC’ALPS since August 2025 as well as a non-cash share based compensation charge of ~$9M.

As of December 31, 2025, SEALSQ held over $425M in cash.

In addition, SEALSQ (LAES) reaffirmed guidance for full-year 2026, with revenue expected to grow between 50%-100% Y/Y.  Q1 2026 revenue is expected to exceed $4M, representing a substantial increase of over 100% on Q1 2025 and signalling accelerated demand across the firm’s traditional product portfolios.

Carlos Moreira, CEO of SEALSQ, stated: “Our 66% revenue growth in 2025, combined with initial expectations of Q1 2026 revenue exceeding $4 million, mark a clear inflection point for SEALSQ. We are transitioning from a development and investment cycle into a structured commercial acceleration phase. With more than $425 million in cash, an estimated $200+ million of new potential revenue opportunities pipeline, and growing global demand for quantum-resistant infrastructure, SEALSQ is strategically positioned to become a cornerstone player in sovereign semiconductor and quantum-security ecosystems worldwide.”

LAES shares are down over 4% at $3.69.

data.title: Most and least shorted tech stocks with up to $2B market cap as of mid-Feb
data.description:
The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) fell nearly 4% by mid-February after a sharp tech-led selloff earlier in the month dragged many leading technology names lower, even as companies reported positive fourth-quarter results.

Against this backdrop, short interest trends among small- and mid-cap tech stocks show a wide divergence. C3.ai (AI) ranks as the most shorted stock, with 29.89% short interest as of Feb. 15, while AMTD Digital Inc. (HKD) stands among the least shorted at 0.51%.

Here’s a list of the 10 most shorted small-to-mid-cap tech stocks as of February 15:

  • C3.ai (AI) – Short interest: 29.89%
  • BigBear.ai Holdings (BBAI) – Short interest: 27.66%
  • indie Semiconductor (INDI) – Short interest: 24.72%
  • Quantum Computing (QUBT) – Short interest: 24.68%
  • Xerox Holdings (XRX) – Short interest: 21.59%

Least shorted small-to-mid-cap tech stocks as of February 15:

  • AMTD Digital (HKD) – Short interest: 0.51%
  • Ebang International (EBON) – Short interest: 0.52%
  • Cognyte Software (CGNT) – Short interest: 0.53%
  • Materialise (MTLS) – Short interest: 0.53%
  • Coda Octopus Group (CODA) – Short interest: 0.54%
